channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Millions of people are pretending to be AI chatbots — for fun
item.description: Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.
item.content:encoded:
Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.

(Image credit: Screenshot by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: The labor economics of 'Alien' — and its lessons for inequality on Earth
item.description: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.content:encoded: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Have we been reading Toni Morrison all wrong?
item.description: Harvard professor Namwali Serpell has been teaching Morrison for nearly two decades. Her book, On Morrison is a deep dive into the Nobel winner's complete body of work — 11 novels, plays and criticism.
item.content:encoded: Harvard professor Namwali Serpell has been teaching Morrison for nearly two decades. Her book, On Morrison is a deep dive into the Nobel winner's complete body of work — 11 novels, plays and criticism.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: 'Speakeasies to Symphonies' and 'Cosmic Music' chronicle 2 jazz greats
item.description: Critic Kevin Whitehead reviews biographies of two musicians who transcended jazz, and to whom recognition was slow in coming: James P. Johnson, born in 1894, and Alice Coltrane, born in 1937.
item.content:encoded: Critic Kevin Whitehead reviews biographies of two musicians who transcended jazz, and to whom recognition was slow in coming: James P. Johnson, born in 1894, and Alice Coltrane, born in 1937.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Whitehead

item.title: Asha Bhosle, the voice of Bollywood, has died aged 92
item.description: The Bollywood legend was one of the world's most recorded artists — who, by her own reckoning, made more than 12,000 songs.
item.content:encoded:
The Bollywood legend was one of the world's most recorded artists — who, by her own reckoning, made more than 12,000 songs.

(Image credit: Steven Siewert)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: The Analog Bag: Gen Z's solution to doomscrolling
item.description: Ayesha Rascoe unpacks an analog bag — a tote filled with screen-free activities — curated by Weekend Edition staff.
item.content:encoded:
Ayesha Rascoe unpacks an analog bag — a tote filled with screen-free activities — curated by Weekend Edition staff.

(Image credit: Dhanika Pineda)
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: This tax season, Goldfish is rewarding parents for the 'Snack Tax'
item.description: As Tax Day approaches, Goldfish offers sneaky snacking parents a special "Snack Tax" refund.
item.content:encoded: As Tax Day approaches, Goldfish offers sneaky snacking parents a special "Snack Tax" refund.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Sweet Treat
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KUT listener Nell Newton and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KUT listener Nell Newton and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
item.description: The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.
item.content:encoded:
The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.

(Image credit: Jonathan Olley)
item.dc:creator: Tara Haelle

item.title: Trump touts newly released plans for D.C. triumphal arch
item.description: The proposed 250-feet-tall, white-and-gilded monument would stand on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., by the Potomac River.
item.content:encoded:
The proposed 250-feet-tall, white-and-gilded monument would stand on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., by the Potomac River.

(Image credit: Jon Elswick)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman
